short_description: A pricing model where scope, timeline, and total cost are clearly defined in advance.
when_to_use: Use when project requirements are very clear, stable, and well‑documented.
key_properties: Cost is agreed upfront; low uncertainty; easier budgeting for clients.

short_description: Pricing based on the time spent and amount of work completed over a period, typically billed monthly.
when_to_use: Use when scope may change over time or needs flexibility during execution.
key_properties: Cost depends on actual effort; scope can increase or decrease; transparent ongoing collaboration.

short_description: Clients hire dedicated developers or a team as an extended development unit and pay for those resources.
when_to_use: Use when client wants a long‑term dedicated team and direct control over workload and priorities.
key_properties: Dedicated resources working only on your project; predictable budget for team, but scope and workload are flexible.
